title: Extract auth context provider from MCP server adapter
body:
## Background

From PR #268 code review finding.

## Problem

The MCP server adapter directly embeds authentication concerns (loading tokens, refreshing, user/tenant accessors, mode gating). This increases coupling between:
- Transport layer (MCP adapter)
- Auth infrastructure

This makes it harder to test and reuse the same auth policy across CLI vs MCP tools.

## Proposed Solution

1. Introduce an `AuthContextProvider` interface in `internal/auth` or as a usecase:
   ```go
   type AuthContextProvider interface {
       Load() (*StoredAuth, error)
       Require() (*StoredAuth, error)  // Like Load but errors if not authenticated
       UserID() string
       TenantID() string
       IsAuthenticated() bool
   }
   ```

2. Inject this provider into the MCP server
3. Keep MCP server focused on tool registration and request handling
4. Reuse the same provider in CLI commands

## Impact

- Cleaner separation of concerns
- Easier testing of auth logic
- Consistent auth behavior across CLI and MCP

## References

- PR #268
- Finding fingerprint: `6b2210505dce6859880292d3a08e4c81`
